sections.paragraph:
pl. גְּבָרים m.-

(1) a man, vir, so called from strength, i.q. אִישׁ, a word with few exceptions ( Deu 22:5 1Ch 24:4 26:12 compare לִגְבָרִים), found only in poetry; in the Aramean (גְּבַר, ܓܒܰܪ, ܓܰܒܪܐܳ) it is very widely used. Psa 34:9, אַשְׁרֵי הַגֶּבֶר יֶחֱסֶה־בֹּו “blessed is the man who trusteth in him;” Psa 52:9 94:12 , etc.; לִגְבָרִים, לַגְּבָרֻים man by man, Jos 7:14, 17 Jos 7:17 1Ch 23:3. Specially-(a) opp. to a woman, a male, Deu 22:5 Jer 30:6 31:22 and even used of male offspring newly born; Job 3:3, “the night which said הֹרָה גֶבֶר a male is conceived;” compare אִישׁ 1, a.-(b) opp. to wife, a husband, Pro 6:34. Sometimes-(c) it denotes the strength of a man, Isa 23:17, “behold Jehovah will cast thee טַלְטֵלָה גֶּבֶר with the casting of a man,” i.e. with a strong, most violent propulsion; Job 38:3 40:7 Psa 88:5 comp. אִישׁ 1, d.-(d) man, homo, opp. to God; compare אִישׁ 1, e, Job 4:17 10:5 14:10 , 14 Job 14:14.-(e) a soldier (compare אִישׁ 1, l), Jud. 5:30 compare Jer 41:16, גְּבָרִים אַנְשֵׁי טִלְחָמָה.

(2) i.q. אִישׁ No. 4, every one, each. Joe 2:8, גֶּבֶר בִּמְסִלָּתֹו יֵלֵכוּן “each one shall go on in his own way.” Lam 3:39 (in the second hemistich).

(3) [Geber], pr.n. m. 1Ki 4:19 comp. 13 1Ki 4:13.
